Name: Effortlessly Invariant Rulesbooks (ERB) - Project Architecture
Description: Meta-rulebook describing the ERB project itself: rulebook-as-hub, project-scoped builds via ssotme-proxy, admin portal UX, and conformance testing across substrates.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Effortlessly Invariant Rulesbooks (ERB)

schema.name: Purpose
schema.Description: Demonstrate that a single declarative rulebook can generate working code in multiple execution substrates that all produce identical results

schema.name: Architecture
schema.Description: Rulebook-as-hub: effortless-rulebook.json is the durable SSoT; Airtable / LLM edits / admin portal are peer input spokes

schema.name: EntryPoint
schema.Description: ./start.sh boots the admin portal (web UI) — local dev experience for any rulebook project

schema.name: PortalUrl
schema.Description: http://localhost:7777 — admin portal served by run-web-portal.sh

schema.name: ProxyUrl
schema.Description: http://localhost:4242 — ssotme-proxy serving substrate transpilers as HTTP routes

schema.name: RepositoryRoot
schema.Description: effortlessly-invariant-rulesbooks/

data.Name: Effortlessly Invariant Rulesbooks
data.Purpose: Multi-substrate code generation from declarative rulebooks; rulebook JSON is the durable SSoT
data.Architecture: effortless-rulebook.json (HUB / durable SSoT) ← input spokes: Airtable, LLM edits, admin portal Postgres editor → output spokes: 10+ substrates via ssotme-proxy

ExecutionSubstrates.Description: Runtime environments that execute business rules derived from the rulebook

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable substrate name

schema.name: Technology
schema.Description: Language/platform (PostgreSQL, Python, Go, Excel, OWL, PlantUML, etc.)

schema.name: RelativePath
schema.Description: Path within repo: execution-substrates/{technology}/

schema.name: InjectorScript
schema.Description: Code generation script: inject-into-{technology}.py

schema.name: TestScript
schema.Description: Conformance test script: take-test.py

schema.name: IsProduction
schema.Description: True if backed by an official Effortless tool; false if local implementation

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Operational status (active, proof-of-concept, deprecated)

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Purpose and capabilities

data.Name: Binary (ARM64)
data.Description: ARM64 assembly proof-of-concept; demonstrates lowest-level code generation

data.Name: Python
data.Description: Local Python implementation: dataclasses + calculated field functions

data.Name: Go
data.Description: Local Go implementation: structs + business logic

data.Name: PostgreSQL
data.Description: Production tool: generates DDL, stored functions, views. Full formula support via SQL.

data.Name: CSV
data.Description: Spreadsheet export: rows + headers

data.Name: Excel
data.Description: Production tool: generates .xlsx with formulas

data.Name: PlantUML
data.Description: Generates UML class diagrams with OCL constraints

data.Name: OWL/RDF
data.Description: RDF/OWL ontology for semantic web

data.Name: Explain DAG
data.Description: JSON spec for calculated-field derivation tracing; embedded React visualizer

data.Name: Entity Framework
data.Description: Production tool: generates C# POCOs and Entity Framework DbContext

OrchestrationComponents.Description: Central orchestration logic that coordinates rulebook loading, injection, and testing

schema.name: FilePath
schema.Description: Relative path from repo root

schema.name: Dependencies
schema.Description: Comma-separated list of other components

data.Name: Start Entry
data.Purpose: Top-level entry point: boots the admin portal (web UI) for the active rulebook project; falls back to legacy CLI menu with --cli flag

data.Name: Shared Utilities
data.Purpose: Common functions: load_rulebook(), parse formulas, type mapping

data.Name: Formula Parser
data.Purpose: Parse and execute Excel-style formulas (IF, AND, OR, CONCAT, LEFT, RIGHT, etc.)

data.Name: Main Injector
data.Purpose: Legacy: dispatch rulebook to all substrate injectors. Superseded by ssotme-proxy + `effortless build` per project, but retained for CI/cleanup.

data.Name: Orchestrator Menu
data.Purpose: Legacy interactive CLI menu: run substrates, manage Airtable sync, generate reports. Reachable via `./start.sh --cli`.

data.Name: Rulebook Cache
data.Purpose: Pull rulebook from an Airtable base into the rulebook JSON; fallback to offline cache if API unavailable. One of several input spokes.

data.Name: Base Manager
data.Purpose: List, select, and swap Airtable bases when Airtable is used as an input spoke

data.Name: Test Orchestrator
data.Purpose: Run all substrate tests; verify conformance (all substrates compute identically)

data.Name: Report Generator
data.Purpose: Generate HTML conformance report comparing substrate outputs

data.Name: Active Domain Pointer
data.Purpose: Single line naming the active rulebook project under rulebook-examples/ — read by start.sh, admin portal, orchestrate.sh

RulebookSourceSpokes.Description: Peer input spokes that can write into effortless-rulebook.json. The rulebook JSON is the durable SSoT; these are interchangeable sources.

schema.name: Kind
schema.Description: airtable | admin-portal | llm-direct | manual-json | reverse-sync

schema.name: Direction
schema.Description: input (writes into rulebook) | output (reads from rulebook) | bidirectional

schema.name: Required
schema.Description: False for all input spokes — any one is sufficient, none is required

schema.name: Authority
schema.Description: On conflict, the rulebook JSON wins; spokes are the editors, not the SSoT

data.Name: Admin Portal (Postgres editor)
data.Purpose: Local web UI; edits flow through Postgres for real-time UX, write-through to rulebook JSON on every save. Primary developer experience.
data.Authority: Rulebook JSON is durable SSoT; Postgres is the live editor only and can be dropped/rebuilt from JSON at any time.

data.Name: Airtable (airtable-to-rulebook)
data.Purpose: Official Effortless tool: pull schema + data from an Airtable base into the rulebook JSON. Optional input spoke (was the historical HEAD).
data.Authority: Rulebook JSON wins; reverse-sync is a separate spoke.

data.Name: Reverse Sync (rulebook-to-airtable)
data.Purpose: Push rulebook JSON edits back to an Airtable base for human review
data.Authority: Output only; never reads from Airtable.

data.Name: LLM Direct Edits
data.Purpose: Claude / agent edits effortless-rulebook.json directly via filesystem; admin portal reflects changes on reload.
data.Authority: Rulebook JSON is the canonical surface for LLM edits.

data.Name: Rulebook Cache (offline)
data.Purpose: Cache of previously-pulled Airtable rulebooks; used when Airtable API unavailable
data.Authority: Last-known-good copy; superseded by any newer authoritative pull.

data.Name: Manual JSON Edits
data.Purpose: Developer hand-edits effortless-rulebook.json in their editor; no special tooling required.
data.Authority: Rulebook JSON is the file under version control.

SsotmeProxy.Description: Local HTTP transpiler server on localhost:4242. Each transpiler is an HTTP route; injectors are the route bodies. Used by `effortless build` to call substrate generators uniformly.

schema.name: Route
schema.Description: POST /{route-name}

schema.name: SubstrateId
schema.Description: FK to ExecutionSubstrates.SubstrateId

schema.name: InjectorScript
schema.Description: Backing script that the route runs; null for upstream Effortless tools (e.g. airtable-to-rulebook)

data.Description: Pull rulebook from Airtable base (delegates to official Effortless tool)
Generate Python dataclass + calc library from rulebook
Generate Go structs + business logic from rulebook
Generate ARM64 assembly calculation stub from rulebook
Generate COBOL computation program from rulebook
Generate CSV exports + rulebook.xlsx from rulebook
Generate Excel workbook with formulas from rulebook
Generate PlantUML class diagram + OCL constraints from rulebook
Generate RDF/OWL ontology from rulebook
Generate plain-English business-rule narrative from rulebook
Generate JSON derivation-tracing DAG spec from rulebook
Sync rulebook schema back into an Airtable base (output spoke)
Health check + transpiler catalog discovery

TestingFramework.Description: Conformance testing: prove all substrates compute identically

schema.name: Scope
schema.Description: global (all substrates) or per-substrate

data.Name: Test Case Definitions
data.Purpose: YAML scenarios defining inputs and expected outputs; shared across all substrates

data.Name: Substrate-Specific Take-Test
data.Purpose: Load rulebook, execute test cases, compare results against expected

data.Name: Test Orchestrator
data.Purpose: Run all substrate tests; aggregate pass/fail; identify conformance gaps

data.Name: Conformance Report
data.Purpose: Visual comparison of substrate outputs; highlight mismatches

RulebookDomains.Description: Customer ontologies: each domain has its own rulebook + substrate generation

schema.name: RelativePath
schema.Description: rulebook-examples/{domain}/ — each domain is a self-contained Effortless project

schema.name: RulebookPath
schema.Description: Path to effortless-rulebook.json within domain

schema.name: ComplexityLevel
schema.Description: minimal, moderate, advanced, or philosophical

schema.name: KeyFeatures
schema.Description: Comma-separated: string concat, relationships, aggregations, IF logic, meta-ontology, etc.

data.Purpose: Hello World: demonstrates basic schema and calculated field (CONCAT formula)
Real workflow modeling: task management with assigned agents

data.KeyFeatures: cross-entity lookups, conditional IF logic, complex aggregations
data.Purpose: Business rule complexity: demonstrates advanced formula patterns
Media catalog: TV shows, seasons, episodes; demonstrates polymorphism
Formal argument modeling: demonstrates schema expressivity for abstract domains

data.KeyFeatures: Hub promotion candidate; demonstrates rulebook-first workflow
data.Purpose: Enterprise demo: ACME Corp operations

data.KeyFeatures: Hub promotion candidate; demonstrates rulebook-first workflow
data.Purpose: Enterprise demo: ACME LLC operations

CoreDataFlows.Description: End-to-end flows from rulebook to execution and testing

schema.name: Steps
schema.Description: Pipe-delimited sequence of steps

schema.name: Triggers
schema.Description: When this flow runs

schema.name: Outputs
schema.Description: Artifacts produced

schema.name: Invariant
schema.Description: Hard rule the flow must preserve (e.g. write-through, SSoT precedence)

data.Name: Start → Admin Portal
data.Steps: User runs ./start.sh | Detect active rulebook project | Start ssotme-proxy on :4242 if not running | Start admin portal backend on :7777 (auto-init Postgres + apply migrations from rulebook) | Open browser to http://localhost:7777
data.Outputs: Live admin portal in browser, ssotme-proxy + backend processes running

data.Name: Project Build (via ssotme-proxy)
data.Steps: User triggers build in admin portal OR runs `effortless build` in active project | effortless CLI walks ProjectTranspilers in active project's effortless.json | For each transpiler with proxy URL, POST rulebook to localhost:4242 | Proxy invokes injector script | Generated files written to project's output folders
data.Triggers: Admin portal Build button, CLI `effortless build`, or post-save hook after rulebook edit

data.Name: Pull from Airtable (optional input spoke)
data.Steps: User chooses 'Pull from Airtable' in admin portal | Call airtable-to-rulebook | Write effortless-rulebook.json | Reload portal Postgres editor from JSON
data.Triggers: Admin portal action OR legacy CLI menu

data.Name: Admin Save (Write-Through Invariant)
data.Steps: Developer edits in portal | Backend writes to Postgres (live editor state) | SAME REQUEST writes to effortless-rulebook.json on disk | Backend returns success only if BOTH writes succeeded | Optional: trigger flow-002 (rebuild substrates)
data.Triggers: Any save action in admin portal (table edit, field edit, formula edit, sample data edit)
data.Outputs: Updated Postgres row, updated effortless-rulebook.json, optional substrate rebuild
data.Invariant: Postgres is the EDITOR; effortless-rulebook.json is the DURABLE SSoT. If Postgres is dropped, the rulebook reconstitutes it. If the JSON is dropped, the editor has no source of truth.

data.Name: Portal Bootstrap (auto-create DB + migrate)
data.Steps: Portal backend boots | Check if PG database 'erb_admin_<project>' exists | If missing, create it | Read effortless-rulebook.json | Run rulebook-to-portal-schema migration (creates tables for rulebook entities + AppUsers + AppRoles + AppNav) | Seed Postgres rows from rulebook JSON | Mark bootstrap complete
data.Triggers: First boot of admin portal for a project, OR explicit reset action
data.Outputs: Postgres database ready for editing, portal navigation populated

data.Name: Rehydrate Postgres from Rulebook JSON
data.Steps: User drops or corrupts Postgres | User runs ./start.sh or clicks Reset Editor | Backend re-runs flow-005 | Editor is back online from JSON SSoT
data.Triggers: Manual reset, DB loss, or version-control checkout to a different rulebook
data.Outputs: Postgres editor rebuilt to match rulebook JSON exactly

data.Name: Conformance Testing
data.Steps: Load test cases YAML | Run each substrate's take-test.py | Collect outputs | Compare results | Generate report | Display in admin portal Tests screen
data.Triggers: Admin portal 'Run Tests' button or CLI option V
data.Outputs: conformance-report.html, pass/fail matrix shown in portal

data.Name: Switch Active Project
data.Steps: User selects a different rulebook project in portal | Update orchestration/active-domain.txt | Reload portal pointing at new project's rulebook | flow-005 if Postgres for that project not yet bootstrapped
data.Triggers: Project switcher in admin portal nav
data.Outputs: Portal now editing the chosen project's rulebook + Postgres editor

data.Name: Clean Generated Files
data.Steps: User triggers Clean in portal Tech Tools OR CLI | Walks ProjectTranspilers | Each route invoked with clean=true | Generated outputs removed
data.Triggers: Tech Tools → Clean Build, or CLI option C

schema.name: MaintainedBy
schema.Description: human (manual edits) or tool (auto-generated)

data.Purpose: SSoTme project definition: transpilers, versions, base IDs, API keys
Project instructions and architecture overview for Claude Code
Catalog of swappable Airtable bases with IDs, names, complexity levels
Environment variables: AIRTABLE_API_KEY, PostgreSQL credentials, etc.
Docker services: PostgreSQL, optional API server
Container image for reproducible environment
DURABLE SSOT: entity schemas, field types, formulas, sample data, admin portal config (roles/nav/screens/AppUsers). Written by any input spoke; read by every output spoke.
data.MaintainedBy: any spoke: admin portal save, airtable-to-rulebook pull, LLM direct edit, or human hand-edit

data.Purpose: Boot ssotme-proxy + admin portal backend + open browser. Invoked by ./start.sh.
Express backend + Vite/React frontend dependencies for the admin portal
Per-machine portal state: last-active project, last-logged-in dev user. Not committed.
User-facing documentation: quick start, base progression, key terms
Technical deep-dive: architecture, internals, debugging

schema.name: Type
schema.Description: Language, tool, service, or external API

data.Name: Python
data.Purpose: Orchestration scripts, injectors, test runners

data.Name: Bash
data.Purpose: Menu orchestration, subprocess management

data.Name: Go
data.Purpose: Go substrate code generation and testing

data.Name: PostgreSQL
data.Purpose: SQL substrate execution; DDL/functions/views generation

data.Name: Docker
data.Purpose: Container orchestration for PostgreSQL and reproducible environments

data.Name: Airtable API
data.Purpose: Schema + data pull via airtable-to-rulebook; reverse sync via rulebook-to-airtable

data.Name: Node.js
data.Purpose: Report generation (HTML), optional utilities

data.Name: SSoTme CLI (effortless)
data.Purpose: Transpiler registry and version management for effortless-rulebook-to-postgres, etc.

data.Name: C# / Entity Framework
data.Purpose: Entity Framework substrate code generation

data.Name: Node.js (Express + Vite + React)
data.Purpose: Admin portal: Express backend, Vite/React frontend. Required to run the default ./start.sh experience.

data.Name: PostgreSQL (admin portal editor)
data.Purpose: Live editor backend for the admin portal. Auto-created on first portal boot. SAFE TO DELETE — reconstitutes from rulebook JSON on next start.

AppUsers.Description: Default users for local development and conformance testing. Lives in the rulebook so it travels with the project; production deployments should overlay these with a real identity provider.

schema.name: Email
schema.Description: Login identifier (no password in dev mode; presence in this list = authorized)

schema.name: RoleId
schema.Description: FK to UserRoles.RoleId (Viewer or Developer)

schema.name: IsDefault
schema.Description: True for the user the portal auto-selects on first boot

data.Notes: Default developer login for ./start.sh. Full admin everywhere.
Read-only login for demos and stakeholder review.
Used by automated conformance tests (CI mode).

UserRoles.Description: Admin portal access tiers. Drives both the navigation visible to the user and the RLS policies that Postgres applies to portal writes.

schema.name: AccessLevel
schema.Description: read | full-admin

schema.name: CanAccessTechTools
schema.Description: Tech Tools nest = raw Postgres explorer, ssotme-proxy logs, conformance internals

data.Name: Viewer
data.Description: Read-only walkthrough of the project: see rulebook, browse generated substrates, view test results. Cannot mutate anything.

data.Name: Developer
data.Description: Full admin: edit rulebook, trigger builds, run tests, manage users, access Tech Tools (raw Postgres, proxy logs, conformance internals).

AppPermissions.Description: RLS-style policy table: declarative per-role allow/deny on portal API endpoints and on Postgres tables. Generated into Postgres on portal bootstrap as RLS policies.

schema.name: RoleId
schema.Description: FK to UserRoles.RoleId

schema.name: Resource
schema.Description: Logical resource: rulebook.entity, rulebook.field, rulebook.formula, build, test, users, tech-tools.postgres, tech-tools.proxy

schema.name: Action
schema.Description: read | create | update | delete | execute

schema.name: RlsPredicate
schema.Description: Postgres RLS USING clause (when applicable); null for endpoint-only checks

AppNavigation.Description: Primary navigation tree for the admin portal. Drives the left sidebar. Each node has a role gate and a target screen. This is the developer's narrative through a rulebook project.

schema.name: ParentNavId
schema.Description: FK to AppNavigation.NavId; null for top-level nodes

schema.name: Icon
schema.Description: Lucide icon name

schema.name: ScreenId
schema.Description: FK to AppScreens.ScreenId; null for grouping-only nodes

schema.name: MinRoleId
schema.Description: FK to UserRoles.RoleId — minimum role required to see this node

schema.name: StoryBeat
schema.Description: What this node teaches the developer in the rulebook narrative

data.Label: Home
data.StoryBeat: What this project is, why it exists, who's working on it. Cards: rulebook stats, last build, last test pass-rate, active spokes.

data.Label: Rulebook
data.StoryBeat: The business semantics of the project — every table, field, formula, and sample row.

data.Label: Entities
data.StoryBeat: List all entities. Click one to drill in to its fields, sample data, and computed columns.

data.Label: Formulas
data.StoryBeat: All calculated fields in one place. Click one to see its DAG (inputs → output) live from the rulebook.

data.Label: Relationships
data.StoryBeat: FK graph of the project. Hover a node to highlight its inbound and outbound relationships.

data.Label: Sample Data
data.StoryBeat: What the project looks like populated. Editable for developers; read-only for viewers.

data.Label: Substrates
data.StoryBeat: Every output substrate this project generates (Python, Go, Postgres, Excel, OWL, etc.). Click one to see the generated source and the conformance status.

data.Label: Add Tool
data.StoryBeat: Pick from the catalog of 15+ transpilers and install one into the active project. Same code path as `effortless -install` on the CLI.

data.Label: Builds
data.StoryBeat: Build history: when, what changed, which substrates regenerated, how long. Developers can trigger a build here.

data.Label: Tests
data.StoryBeat: Conformance matrix: which substrate computed which test case correctly. Drill in to see input → expected → actual per substrate.

data.Label: Input Spokes
data.StoryBeat: Where edits come from: admin portal, Airtable, LLM, manual JSON. Pull / push controls live here.

data.Label: Users
data.StoryBeat: Default dev/test users from the rulebook + their roles.

data.Label: Tech Tools
data.StoryBeat: Developer-only escape hatches. Raw Postgres, proxy logs, file system, manual injection. Not part of the daily workflow.

data.Label: Postgres Explorer
data.StoryBeat: Raw editor-DB browser: run SQL, inspect rows, drop/reset DB.

data.Label: ssotme-proxy
data.StoryBeat: Live proxy status: registered routes, recent calls, response sizes, last error per route.

data.Label: Files
data.StoryBeat: Project filesystem browser. View any generated or hand-written file in the active project.

data.Label: Rulebook JSON
data.StoryBeat: Raw rulebook JSON viewer/editor. Save here goes through the same write-through invariant as the UI.

data.Label: Reset Editor
data.StoryBeat: Drop the editor Postgres DB and re-bootstrap from rulebook JSON. Safe — JSON is SSoT.

AppScreens.Description: Every screen in the admin portal. Each screen names the entities it reads/writes, the role it requires, and the story it tells.

schema.name: Path
schema.Description: React Router path

schema.name: ReadsEntities
schema.Description: Comma-separated rulebook entities the screen reads

schema.name: WritesEntities
schema.Description: Comma-separated rulebook entities the screen mutates (developer-only)

schema.name: Layout
schema.Description: split-detail | list | grid | dashboard | editor

schema.name: Story
schema.Description: What the developer learns / does on this screen

data.Title: Home
data.Story: Land here on ./start.sh. Cards: current project, rulebook size, # substrates, last build time, last test pass-rate, which spokes are active, who you're signed in as.

data.Title: Entities
data.Story: Left: list of every entity in the active project's rulebook. Right: selected entity's fields, formulas, sample rows. Developer can add/rename/delete fields inline; viewer sees the same with controls disabled.

data.Title: Formulas
data.Story: Every calculated field across all entities. Drill in to see live DAG visualization (inputs → intermediates → output) sourced from the rulebook's explain-dag substrate. Developer can edit Excel-style formula in place; portal auto-validates against the parser.

data.Title: Relationships
data.Story: FK graph (interactive). Hover a node to highlight its inbound/outbound edges. Click an edge to see which formula or column declares it.

data.Title: Sample Data
data.Story: Spreadsheet-style view of the data block of each entity. Developer can edit cells; computed columns are read-only and show the resolved value with a hoverable derivation popover.

data.Title: Substrates
data.Story: Left: every substrate this project emits (with last-build timestamp + conformance status). Right: file tree of generated output + a preview pane for any file. Developer can trigger 'Rebuild just this substrate'.

data.Title: Add Tool
data.Story: Browse the 15+ transpilers in the catalog. Click one, choose output path, hit Install. Portal shells out to `effortless -install <proxy-url>` so the result is byte-identical to the CLI path. Tool then shows up in /substrates and is ready for the next build.

data.Title: Builds
data.Story: Chronological list of builds. Click one to see which transpilers ran, duration each, stdout/stderr per route, and which files changed. Trigger-build button is gated by CanRunBuilds.

data.Title: Tests
data.Story: Matrix: rows = test cases, columns = substrates, cells = pass/fail. Click a failing cell to see input, expected, actual, and a diff. Trigger-tests button is gated by CanRunBuilds.

data.Title: Input Spokes
data.Story: List of every configured input spoke for this project. Status (last-pulled time, last-error). Developer can trigger a pull from any spoke.

data.Title: Users
data.Story: Default dev/test users from the rulebook + roles. Developer can add new users (saved through the write-through invariant, so they end up in rulebook JSON).

data.Title: Postgres Explorer
data.Story: Raw SQL console + table browser for the editor Postgres DB. Developer-only escape hatch. Background banner reminds: rulebook JSON is the SSoT, this DB is rebuildable.

data.Title: ssotme-proxy
data.Story: Live status of localhost:4242: registered routes, recent calls (route, duration, status, response size), last error per route. Restart button.

data.Title: Project Files
data.Story: Browse the active project folder. View any file. Read-only — prevents drift from rulebook.

data.Title: Raw Rulebook JSON
data.Story: Monaco editor on the raw effortless-rulebook.json. Save goes through the write-through invariant. Useful for bulk edits.

data.Title: Reset Editor
data.Story: One-button reset: drops the editor Postgres DB and re-bootstraps from rulebook JSON. The reassurance screen — makes the JSON-as-SSoT promise tangible.

AppAPIs.Description: Admin portal HTTP API surface. Express routes mounted by the portal backend.

AppUsers from active rulebook

Remove user. Write-through.

Available transpilers to add to active project. Sources: localhost:4242/ping (local) + Effortless registry. Drives the Add Tool screen.
Currently installed transpilers from active project's effortless.json (ProjectTranspilers array).
Add a tool to the active project: shells out to `effortless -install <proxy-url> -i <rulebook>`. Same CLI path the orchestrator uses — guarantees portal/CLI parity.
Toggle IsDisabled on a ProjectTranspilers entry.
Remove a tool from active project's effortless.json.

BuildPipeline.Description: The effortless.json contract that both the admin portal and the CLI consume. There is ONE pipeline definition per project; both surfaces are thin wrappers around `effortless build` so they stay in lockstep.

schema.name: PortalLocation
schema.Description: Where in the portal this aspect surfaces

schema.name: Authority
schema.Description: File or system that holds the canonical state

data.Authority: Both invoke the same `effortless -install` command — portal shells out so behaviour matches CLI exactly.
effortless CLI walks ProjectTranspilers and POSTs to each proxy URL
Proxy receives clean=true; each transpiler clears its output dir
Local: GET /ping on ssotme-proxy. Remote: Effortless registry (effortless catalog).

AdminPortalRuntime.Description: Runtime processes that ./start.sh boots and supervises.

data.Name: ssotme-proxy
data.Purpose: Substrate transpiler HTTP server. Started before backend so build endpoints work immediately.

data.Name: admin-portal-backend
data.Purpose: Express API + static frontend host. Owns the write-through invariant.

data.Name: admin-portal-frontend-dev
data.Purpose: Vite dev server in dev mode. In production mode the backend serves built static files from port 7777.

data.Name: postgres
data.Command: docker compose up -d postgres (or system pg)
data.Purpose: Editor database. Auto-init on first portal boot via flow-005.

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Meta-rulebook describing the ERB project itself: rulebook-as-hub SSoT, ssotme-proxy (10+ substrate routes), admin portal (Viewer/Developer roles, navigation, screens, APIs, write-through invariant), AppUsers + permissions, build pipeline, conformance testing.
_meta._ProjectGoal: Demonstrate that a single declarative rulebook (effortless-rulebook.json) can generate working, conformant code in 10+ execution substrates that all produce identical results — AND that the developer experience on top of it (the admin portal) is itself driven entirely by rulebook entities (navigation, roles, screens, permissions, users).
_meta._ArchitecturalHighlight: effortless-rulebook.json is the DURABLE SSoT. Input spokes (admin portal, Airtable, LLM, manual JSON) all write into it. Output spokes (Python, Go, Postgres, Excel, OWL, PlantUML, Explain-DAG, English, COBOL, ARM64, Entity Framework, Airtable reverse-sync) all read from it. Every substrate passes the same conformance tests.
_meta._WriteThroughInvariant: The admin portal's Postgres database is the LIVE EDITOR; the rulebook JSON is the DURABLE SSOT. Every save in the portal must write to BOTH (in the same logical transaction). If Postgres is dropped, the editor rebuilds from JSON. If JSON is lost, the editor has no source of truth.
_meta._PortalCliParity: The admin portal and ./start.sh --cli are peer interfaces to the SAME effortless.json pipeline. Install Tool, Build, Clean, Test, and project switching all shell out to the same `effortless` CLI commands — so portal behaviour cannot drift from CLI behaviour.
_meta._BootstrapStory: Cold start: `git clone && ./start.sh` → start.sh boots ssotme-proxy on :4242, starts portal backend on :7777, backend auto-creates editor Postgres DB if missing and runs migrations derived from the rulebook, opens browser. Developer signs in as the default user from the rulebook's AppUsers entity. Land on Home. The whole story unfolds from one command.
_meta._DeveloperJourney: Home → pick a project (or accept active one) → Rulebook to see what the project means → Substrates to see what it generates → Builds to see how it built → Tests to verify conformance → Input Spokes to see where edits come from → Users to see who can edit. Tech Tools only for escape-hatch debugging.
_meta._ResilienceClaim: Drop the entire Postgres editor any time. ./start.sh rebuilds it from JSON. The rulebook JSON travels with the repo; the editor DB does not.
